Design system · Prism · adopted July 2026

Paper and ink,
and eight feelings in color

This is the shipped dyeary design system, generated from the app source. One voice (Chillax), sandy paper over a near-black night, monochrome ink for every control, and exactly one source of color: the eight moods. Every token carries the same name it has in Theme.swift, so point at anything here by name and the change ships everywhere at once.

Source of truth: dyeary/dyeary/Theme.swift · regenerate this page with design/build-design-system.py · toggle the theme to proof the dark ramp
01

The horizon mark

A hairline sun on a horizon, half risen. Ochre ring and horizon line, tan half-disc below.

Primary mark

Assets.xcassets/logo.horizon · logo-light.svg / logo-dark.svg
Rendered via HorizonMotif (default 118 pt). The asset ships its own dark variant in the assigned dark ochres; it is never template-tinted. Ask before authoring any new cut of the mark.

On the scrim

recording, streak and coach overlays
Overlay surfaces are fixed near-black (overlayScrim) in both themes, so the dark cut of the mark lives there permanently.
02

Color

Sandy paper, near-black navy ink, and a monochrome accent that is ink, never a hue. Dark mode is near-black with all color carried by the moods. Tap any swatch or hex to copy it.

Paper · surfaces

Ink · text and lines

Accent · monochrome ink

Functional

Fixed overlay palette · identical in both themes

Semantic aliases · what code actually calls

bgApppaper1
surfaceCardpaper0
surfaceListpaper0 light · paper2 dark
surfaceRaisedpaper2
surfaceSunkenpaper3
textHeadingink800
textBodyink700
textMutedink500
textSubtleink300
textOnDarkpaper0
accentaccent600
motifFillink900
03

The eight moods

Every feeling the model detects folds into one of eight buckets, and the moods are the only color in the app: saturated spectrum inks, except calm, the one low-arousal feeling, which gets the quietest color in the family. Each mood also owns a piano chord, all voiced in C major inside one octave, so any two played together stay consonant.

Tap a mood to hear its chord, played on the same piano sample the app uses (piano_d5.wav, pitched per note).
04

One voice

Adopted: a single variable family, Chillax, carries every word in the app, from the splash screen to the diary itself. Hierarchy comes from size and one step of weight, never from switching faces.

Chillax · variabledesign/assets/Chillax-Variable.ttf · wght 200 to 700 · Fontshare FF EULA, app embedding allowed
A quiet record of how the days felt
ExtralightLight350RegularMediumSemibold
Semibold is the ceiling: heavier weights exist in the file but never render in the app. There is no italic cut, and none is synthesized; the old italic signature is now a color move (the accent span in the specimen above, upright). Being variable, in-between weights like 350 are real, not faked.
Roleshow one family covers the four old voices
RoleSizeWeightWhere
Display30 to 46 ptlight, 300 to 350greetings, screen titles, hero and paywall moments; bigger gets lighter
Titles19 to 24 ptmedium 500entry titles, feed-card titles, category names
Diary17 to 19 ptregular 400entries, transcripts, reflections; generous leading, never used for chrome
UI15 to 17 ptregular 400, medium 500 on buttons and chipscontrols, tabs, sheets
Metadata11 to 13 ptregular 400 to medium 500timestamps, eyebrows; small-caps labels are 11 pt medium with 1.6 tracking
Emphasis is one step of weight, never two. Numerals that align in columns get monospaced digits.
RetiredCormorant · Newsreader · Hanken Grotesk
Cormorant (display), Newsreader (reading) and Hanken Grotesk (UI) left the app bundle in the Prism migration; their token values are archived in the previous-system section below. The one exception that survives every version: the Apple sign-in button keeps the system font because Apple requires it, and the Google button beside it matches at SF 19 medium.
05

Space and shape

A 4 px rhythm for spacing, one continuous-corner radius scale for everything rounded.

Spacing · Theme.Space

values in points
gutter (18) is the screen-edge padding on every screen.

Radii · Theme.Radius

continuous (squircle) corners everywhere
Cards sit on lg (22). Sheets and the paywall use xl to xxl. pill (999) is every capsule button.
06

Elevation

Shadows are navy-black on paper (#101228), pure black on the night ground, always cast downward. Three steps only.

sm
opacity .10 · radius 5 · y 3
rows, small chips, pressed FAB
md
opacity .16 · radius 14 · y 8
the default card shadow (dsShadow)
lg
opacity .22 · radius 26 · y 14
tab bar, floating record button
07

Paper grain

Every screen sits on cream paper with a tiled noise texture in Overlay blend (dsPaper). Overlay blend dies on a near-black base, so dark mode screens faint light grain in instead, like film.

bare bgApp
dsPaper · light · overlay 0.90
dsPaper · dark · screen 0.08

The blend is isolated with a compositing group so the grain never tints content, only the paper behind it. Source tile: Assets.xcassets/noise_overlay.

08

Icons

Two tiers, on purpose: glass icons (baked from the nucleo-glass playground in navy ink over sand glass) are the expressive tier for tabs, the FAB and feature moments; line icons (template-tinted, 18 pt default) are the control tier for chevrons, plus, xmark and other micro-controls. Unselected icons tint iconMuted, a neutral grey, never a hue.

The Prism recut · baked from the nucleo-glass playground

The shipping glass set rebaked in the v3 palette (navy ink body, sand glass, paper sheen), the streak sun in amber, accent variants in viridian, and five line icons promoted to glass: bell, gear, lock, warning, paperplane. Colors come from the playground presets, which now mirror the v3 tokens; rebake with bake-prism.mjs after any token change. Still line-only by design: chevrons, plus, xmark, play, pause, threedot (micro-controls), plus clock, envelope, trash, bookmark (no honest glass equivalent in the package).

09

Components

The primitives everything is assembled from. Demos are live HTML approximations; the source of truth is the Swift on the right of each caption.

DSCard

Components.swift · paper card, radius lg, hairlineSoft stroke, md shadow
Tuesday evening
Long walk after the studio, everything felt lighter.
With motif
CardMotif: filled quarter-disc (r 40, motifFill) under a hairline arc (r 78), clipped by the corner.

Glass card

DSCard(glass: true) · ultraThinMaterial + paper0 at 45%
Over a mood wash
Glass cards float over colored or photographic ground, with a bright hairline.

Buttons

LoginView, PaywallView · capsules, height 56
Primary is an ink900 capsule with paper0 text, so it flips to cream-on-espresso in dark mode. Selection chips fill with ochre600. Every one of these presses with HapticPressStyle.

Floating record button

HomeView · FloatingButtonStyle
Rests at lg elevation, same as the tab bar. Pressing scales to 0.9 and drops the shadow to sm, so it reads as physically pushed down.

Week strip

WeekStripView · streak flame + six day discs
4
Fri3
Sat4
Sun5
Mon6
Tue7
Wed8
The active day fills with its entry's mood color (caramel when empty). Days with entries are paper0 discs; empty days are dashed hairline over paper2. The leftmost slot is the streak sun with the day count above it.

Mood square

MoodSquare · slice-and-dice treemap, 40 pt, corner 11
Each entry's feeling blend as a tiny treemap: area is proportional to weight, the largest mood splits along the longer axis first. The leading marker for every feed row.

Small-caps label

DSLabel · ui 11 semibold, tracking 1.6, ink300
This week in feelings Reflections
The section voice of the whole app. It stays ink; nothing decorative is ever colored.

Callout pill

EntryDetailView HUD · fixed calloutDark + calloutText in both themes
Joy · while this part plays Entry saved
Floating status over content. Deliberately never flips with the theme, so it reads as the same object everywhere.
10

The key screens

Four screens rebuilt from the shipped SwiftUI, wired to the same tokens as everything above. Change a token and these re-skin with it; flip the theme to proof the espresso versions. Sizes are true points at iPhone width.

Shipped the export feature at midnight. Bed is the whole plan.

She asked about the app again and actually remembered the name this time.
8:03 PMLove
Home feed · HomeView.swift. Pinned week strip, display greeting with the italic ochre name, day groups as 2-up mood-tinted cards (tint = mood color mixed 18% into paper1), glass tab pill + ink900 record FAB.
9:41
July 8 · Overall calm
Rain on the studio roof
Subjects Transcript Edit
The rain started right as I left the studio, which felt almost scripted.
I keep thinking about how the paywall finally feels like part of the app instead of a gate bolted on.
Dan texted that his whole team wants invites, which never happens.
Tomorrow I want to clean up the dark colors before the next build.
Calm
Entry detail · EntryDetailView.swift, read-along state. Cormorant title with mood eyebrow, playing audio ring, Subjects/Transcript underline tabs, per-sentence mood rules; the spoken sentence carries the current words at full ink while everything else sits at 50%. HUD pill + scrubbing dots pinned under a paper fade.
9:41
30 seconds left
Just following up…
Did the rain walk help clear your head?
2:41
Calm Tired
RECORDING
Recording overlay · RecordingOverlay.swift. The scrim is the live mood color mixed 82% into overlayScrim and retints as you talk; follow-up cards in cream glass, decorative equalizer, live feeling pills. The top warning pill is the [NEEDS DESIGN] surface.
9:41
Reflections
The quotes that make up your day to day
Work 3 categories
dyeary Dan +1 more
Love 2 categories
Mom Sunday calls
Health 2 categories
Morning walks Sleep
View All Categories
Reflections · InsightsView.swift. Display title with Newsreader subtitle, six centered life-area cards (three shown) tinted by each area's mood, category chips on paper0 capsules with mood dots, ochre text button to the full categories screen.
11

Motion, touch and sound

One spring for everything: response 0.28, damping 0.6. Every button gives touch feedback, and most give a light haptic on press-down.

StylePress behaviorUsed on
HapticPressStylescale 0.96 + opacity 0.85 + light impact hapticthe default for every button; scale eases toward 0.99 for large surfaces
PressScaleStylescale 0.93, no haptic in the styleentry tiles, timeline pills
CandlePressStylescale 0.86 + opacity 0.55the streak sun
FloatingButtonStylescale 0.9 + shadow morphs lg to smthe record FAB

Sound: each mood strikes a three-note chord from one key (C major) inside one octave (D4 to E5), sampled from a single piano note and pitched per chord tone. No tritones, no diminished chords, so scrubbing the timeline glides instead of jarring. Playback keeps the screen awake; mood changes during playback stay silent.

12

Where it lives

Change the token, and every screen follows. Nothing here is defined twice.

Theme.swiftevery color, mood, font lookup, spacing, radius and shadow token
Components.swiftDSLabel, DSCard, CardMotif, HorizonMotif, WeekStripView, MoodSquare, Icon, all press styles
Grain.swiftdsPaper, the paper + noise background
Haptics.swiftimpact helpers behind the press styles
MoodAudio.swiftthe piano sampler and mood chords
Fonts/Chillax statics: Light, Regular, Medium, Semibold (the ceiling)
Assets.xcassetslogo.horizon, streak.sun, noise_overlay, ic.* / tab.* / area.* icon sets
design/design-system.htmlthis document · regenerate with design/build-design-system.py
13

The previous system

Archived for the record: what dyeary looked like before Prism (retired July 2026), and why it changed. The goal of the pass was a personality of our own; cream paper, a caramel accent and an elegant serif were the recognizable AI-default combination.

What changedthe de-Claude pass, resolved
Type: Cormorant (display), Newsreader (reading) and Hanken Grotesk (UI) collapsed into one voice, Chillax, with weights one notch down and a semibold ceiling. The italic-ochre signature became weight-and-ink emphasis.
Ground: warm cream (#F4EFE4) became sandy ivory (#F6F1E4); espresso dark (#1C1813) became near-black night (#0A0B10).
Ink: warm charcoal (#211C16) became near-black navy (#171C3A ramp).
Accent: caramel ochre600 (#8A5A24 / #CB9450) went to blue, then viridian, and settled as monochrome ink; controls are never a hue.
Moods: the muted set (joy #D99A2B, calm sage #7E9B79, love #C58A86, sad #6E8198, anger #B5523C, fear #8A7BA0, surprise #E0B255, tired #9B948A) was remapped onto a saturated spectrum from the brand palette, with pine calm as the one quiet ink.
Icons: the glass set rebaked from ochre to navy ink over sand glass; five line icons promoted to glass.
Kept: the horizon mark and area discs (still ochre, pending their own recut), the grain, the mood chords, the treemap squares, the spacing and radii scales, every component's anatomy.
dyeary design system · generated from the app source on July 10, 2026 · repo: design/design-system.html · tokens: dyeary/dyeary/Theme.swift
